system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 28.

Size: Varies. Point Cost: 0.

Rules Summary: A supported zero-cost build path for characters who identify with a Race but do not allocate Points to a fixed Race package.
system.traitNotes: Use this option when the character saves Race Points for discretionary Attributes instead of receiving a predefined Race package. The character can still identify with a Race or unique ancestry in narrative terms.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 30.

Size: Huge. Point Cost: 15.

Rules Summary: Huge fiend template built around extreme Strength, fast movement, darkvision, damage conversion, protection, tunnelling, and large-size drawbacks.

abilityBonuses.notes: Charisma drawback from the race cost table.

system.traitNotes: Aerial Archfiends gain Flight Rank 2 (90 feet/round) and typically lose Conversion.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 31.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 11.

Rules Summary: Winged stewards with Dexterity, Intelligence, and Charisma bonuses, flight, woodland languages, danger sense, and Zen Direction.

attributes.name: Flight
attributes.notes: Small agile wings need little room to manoeuvre.

attributes.name: Sixth Sense
attributes.notes: Wings vibrate near harmful creatures, conditions, or environments.

attributes.name: Special Movement
attributes.notes: The land guides their movement when they are open to receiving guidance.

movement.notes: Base flying speed from the Flight trait.

system.traitNotes: Blessed Asrai subrace variation adds Dynamic Power (Nature) Rank 1.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 32.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 10.

Rules Summary: Forest-linked shapeshifters with a human alternate identity, lesser vegetation powers, teleportation, Sylvan/Common language, and social/agility drawbacks.

abilityBonuses.notes: Dexterity drawback from the race cost table.
Charisma drawback from the race cost table.

system.traitNotes: Multi Blinkbeasts have Alternate Identity Rank 6 total, representing six humanoid forms.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 33.

Size: Large. Point Cost: 14.

Rules Summary: Large fire-resistant serpent folk with strong melee traits, magic-save edge, fast movement, protection, thrown-distance benefits, and large-body penalties.

abilityBonuses.notes: Wisdom drawback from the race cost table.
Charisma drawback from the race cost table.

system.traitNotes: Aqua Demonaga gain Sixth Sense (Water) Rank 1 and Water Speed Rank 3 (300 feet/round); they are mortal enemies of Fire Demonaga.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 34.

Size: Diminutive. Point Cost: 4.

Rules Summary: Tiny flying folk with strong defence, attack accuracy, light magic, keen smell, languages, and major strength, damage, speed, and vulnerability limits.

movement.notes: Base flying speed from the Flight trait.

system.traitNotes: Rock Fairies are Tiny instead of Diminutive and also gain Constitution +1.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 35.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 12.

Rules Summary: Intelligent alien-like folk with ambidexterity, darkvision, ultrasonic communication, heightened senses, basic mind control, and healing magic.
system.traitNotes: Elite Grey have Mind Control Rank 3 with the Area Enhancement -2, affecting one target in a 30-foot radius.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 36.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 13.

Rules Summary: Draconic heritage package with Strength and Constitution bonuses, flight, lesser fire immunity, Common/Draconic language, and fire breath.

attributes.name: Weapon
attributes.detail: Fire Breath; 2d6 damage; Continuing -1; Range: 30 ft -2; Spreading: 3 targets -2; Save +4
attributes.notes: Listed as Rank 4 with effective Rank 3 in the source table.

movement.notes: Base flying speed from the Flight trait.

system.traitNotes: Gold Half-Dragons are most common. Bronze, Copper, and Silver variants change the Lesser Immunity and breath Weapon to lightning, acid, or cold.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 37.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 9.

Rules Summary: Regenerative folk with Wisdom, darkvision, smell, several languages, strong regeneration, and a Constitution drawback.

abilityBonuses.notes: Constitution drawback from the race cost table.

attributes.name: Regeneration
attributes.notes: Regenerates bodily trauma normally, including severe injuries over several days.

system.traitNotes: Water Half-Troll subrace variation trades darkvision for Heightened Senses (Taste), adds Resilient (Underwater) Rank 1, and gains Water Speed Rank 2 (90 feet/round).

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 38.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 12.

Rules Summary: Aggressive reptilian folk with Intelligence, initiative edge, bonus action, darkvision, heightened senses, poison resistance, wall-crawling, and cold-related weaknesses.

abilityBonuses.notes: Charisma drawback from the race cost table.

movement.notes: Claws and tail allow movement along all but the smoothest vertical surfaces.

system.traitNotes: Leaping Haud have Strength +2 and Jumping Rank 2, allowing jumps up to 10 times normal distance.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 39.

Size: Tiny. Point Cost: 10.

Rules Summary: Small spirit folk with Wisdom, strong AC and attack bonuses, state changing, postcognition, banishment/healing magic, and size-based drawbacks.
system.traitNotes: Void Kodama replace Cure Wounds with Nullify Rank 2 against magic-sourced Attributes.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 40.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 8.

Rules Summary: Catlike folk with Dexterity, initiative edge, darkvision, hearing, rerolls, cat-like movement, and a feline distraction Defect.
system.traitNotes: Panthera gain Strength +1, Constitution +1, and Weapon: Fangs Rank 2 (1d6 + Strength Bonus damage), but do not possess Mulligan.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 41.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 16.

Rules Summary: Symbiotic combat template with elastic limbs, a limited bonus action, lightning immunity, enhanced unarmed damage, extending blades, and loud-sound weakness.
system.traitNotes: Parasites have no subraces. Since they bond to a host body, characters often have two Races assigned, such as Parasite and Human or Parasite and Fairy.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 42.

Size: Medium. Point Cost: 7.

Rules Summary: Nimble folk with Constitution and Charisma, a limited bonus action, fast movement, jumping, languages, and an easily distracted Defect.
system.traitNotes: Forest Satyrs gain Weapon Rank 2 horns that inflict 2d4 + Strength Bonus damage. Under Satyrs gain Features (Darkvision 60 ft) Rank 1 and Immunity - Lesser (Acid) Rank 2.

system.description:
Source: Anime 5E Fifth Edition Core Rules, p. 43.

Size: Small. Point Cost: 11.

Rules Summary: Elastic small folk with Constitution and Charisma, AC and attack bonuses, regeneration, slithering, and several size, strength, speed, and slime-trail drawbacks.

movement.notes: Maintains a low profile while moving across land.

system.traitNotes: The table represents the common Blue Slime. Variant colours and sizes may exhibit Features, Flight, Healing, Immunity, Mimic, Mind Control, Size Change, Supersense, Telepathy, or Weapon.
